cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: 6sense Launches MCP Server, Exposing GTM Intelligence to Any AI Agent
stories.plain: 6sense opens an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so any AI agent — Claude, ChatGPT, Agentforce, Writer — can pull its predictive buying-stage and account-intent data at runtime. This matters because it turns a closed data silo into a callable tool, letting agentic workflows make smarter, real-time GTM decisions without custom integrations.

stories.head: OneSignal Unveils Autonomous Lifecycle Marketing Vision and Opens AI + MCP Server to All Users
stories.plain: OneSignal, used by over a million businesses, announces Autonomous Lifecycle Marketing: a self-improving platform that plans, executes, and optimises messaging campaigns without manual setup. It also opens its AI and MCP server to all users today. This signals a shift from rule-based CEPs to fully agentic engagement systems.

stories.head: Profound Launches FactCheck to Measure Brand Accuracy Inside AI Answer Engines
stories.plain: Profound's new FactCheck tool lets brands identify and measure factual errors in how AI search engines describe them, then take steps to correct those errors at scale. As AI answers replace traditional search results for many queries, getting your brand represented accurately in those answers is becoming a core marketing task.

stories.head: Run This AI Audit Before Your Next Budgeting Cycle
stories.plain: A practical framework for marketing teams to honestly assess where AI is actually adding value versus where it is just hype before committing budget. Useful for non-specialists because it moves past benchmarks and gives a step-by-step internal evaluation process that affects how AI tools get funded and governed.

stories.head: DataGroomr Adds Real-Time Cross-Object Duplicate Prevention on Salesforce AgentExchange
stories.plain: DataGroomr now catches duplicate records across related Salesforce objects the moment they are created, and lists on Salesforce AgentExchange — the new marketplace combining AppExchange, Slack, and Agentforce. Clean data at ingestion is a prerequisite for reliable agentic decisions, making this a governance story as much as a data-quality one.

stories.head: 6sense Launches MCP Server, Bringing Proprietary GTM Intelligence Into Any AI Agent
stories.plain: 6sense has opened its predictive buying-stage and account-intent data to any AI agent — Claude, ChatGPT, Agentforce and others — via a Model Context Protocol server. This is a structural shift: third-party agents can now act on 6sense intelligence without custom integrations, changing how B2B go-to-market AI is built and bought.

stories.head: OneSignal Unveils Autonomous Lifecycle Marketing Vision and Opens AI and MCP Server to All Users
stories.plain: OneSignal, used by over a million businesses, is repositioning its customer engagement platform around autonomous lifecycle marketing — a self-improving system that plans and executes onboarding, engagement, and retention campaigns on its own. Making its AI and MCP server free to all users raises the floor for what a CEP is expected to do.

stories.head: Profound Launches FactCheck to Measure Brand Accuracy Across AI Answer Engines
stories.plain: Profound's new FactCheck tool lets marketing teams track and correct how AI search engines describe their brand — identifying inaccurate claims at scale. As consumers increasingly trust AI-generated answers over search results, being represented accurately inside those answers is becoming a core marketing governance problem.

stories.head: Omneky Launches Public API and MCP Server for Autonomous Ad Creative Generation
stories.plain: Omneky's new public API and MCP server let any developer or AI agent call its autonomous ad-creative generation engine directly. This opens a new procurement path — teams can embed AI creative production into their own tools and agents rather than logging into a separate platform, reshaping how creative AI is built into stacks.

stories.head: AI Adoption Hits 90% in CX but Organizations Are Divided on Architecture and Governance
stories.plain: A new report finds AI use in customer experience is near-universal, but organizations are splitting sharply on how to govern it, which architecture to trust, and how to prove ROI. For buyers and builders, this data exposes where the real risk now sits — not in adoption, but in the choices made after adoption.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: MCP Servers Make AI Agents Composable Infrastructure
body: Within a single month, 6sense, OneSignal, and Omneky all shipped MCP servers — a pattern that signals a structural shift, not a product cycle. Proprietary data and creative engines are being wrapped as callable services so any AI agent, regardless of vendor, can invoke them on demand.

The decision this forces for marketing ops leaders is architectural: do you build a stack of closed platforms, or assemble an agent mesh where best-in-class capabilities plug in as modules? MCP is quietly becoming the API standard layer for agentic MarTech, and vendors who delay will find their data locked out of the workflows that matter.
ev: MarTech Series · OneSignal MCP + autonomous lifecycle
Agile Brand · Omneky MCP & public API

themes.head: AI Search Visibility Replaces Classic SEO as Brand Battleground
body: When AI assistants answer questions about your brand, they draw on a corpus of signals that classic keyword rankings do not fully capture. Trust, authority, and factual accuracy in third-party sources now determine whether a brand appears — or is misrepresented — in AI-generated responses.

Profound's FactCheck launch makes brand accuracy in AI answers measurable for the first time, turning an abstract concern into a trackable metric. Marketers who treat AI answer-engine optimization as a distinct discipline alongside traditional SEO will have an early-mover advantage before the category crowds.

themes.head: The CMS Evolves Into an AI Brand Operating System
body: The content management system is no longer just a publishing tool — it is becoming the central nervous system that feeds structured brand knowledge to AI agents, search engines, and personalization layers. This reframes CMS selection from a UX decision to an infrastructure decision with long-term AI consequences.

Separately, mainframe-to-modern CX modernization stories underscore the same pressure: legacy content & data architectures create bottlenecks the moment AI agents need to query them at speed. Brands that have not rationalized their content layer will find AI-driven personalization stalling at the data retrieval step.
ev: MarTech · 3 AI agents for marketing workflows

themes.head: AI Governance & Audit Pressure Arrives at Budget Time
body: C-suite pressure and tightening budgets are colliding with AI tool sprawl, forcing marketers to justify every platform investment with measurable outcomes. Running a structured AI audit before the next budget cycle is no longer optional hygiene — it is the prerequisite for securing headcount and spend approvals.

Vendor due-diligence is becoming equally formalised: MarTech's five-question framework for AI vendors and Hyperlink InfoSystem's risk-reduction guidance both reflect a market maturing from experimentation to accountable, risk-managed deployment. Buyers who skip this step face both wasted spend and regulatory exposure.
ev: MarTech Series · C-suite pressure on digital strategy

themes.head: AI Raises the Talent & Skills Gap to a Strategic Risk
body: As AI absorbs routine campaign execution, the junior roles that historically trained the next generation of marketers are disappearing — creating a pipeline problem that will surface as a leadership shortage in three to five years. The question of who builds institutional knowledge is no longer theoretical.

The same dynamic is visible in AI CX adoption data: 90% adoption rates mask a widening gap between organizations that are thoughtfully upskilling teams versus those automating blindly. Leaders who treat workforce development as a lagging indicator will find themselves without the human judgment needed to govern the AI systems they have deployed.
ev: MarTech · AI adoption hits 90% in CX
MarTech · scaling vibe coding beyond prompts

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack — infrastructure, content, search, governance, and talent — the shift from AI as a tool to AI as the operating environment is the single decision that will separate durable marketing organisations from those optimising yesterday's playbook.
